mappings.rationaleAI: Newton's three laws of motion (inertia, force, action-reaction), universal gravitation, and the synthesis of terrestrial and celestial mechanics comprehensively address statics (A.2), dynamics (A.3), Newton's laws (A.3.b.i), and gravitational law (A.3.b.v).

Special and general relativity covering spacetime unification, E=mc2, time dilation, and gravitational lensing directly address relativistic mechanics (C), including mass-energy equivalence (A.3.e.iii).
Kepler's three laws of planetary motion (elliptical orbits, equal areas, harmonic law) directly address Kepler's laws (A.3.b.vi) and celestial mechanics (B).

The speed of light as a fundamental constant of relativity, time dilation, length contraction, and mass-energy equivalence address mechanical foundations of special relativity (C.1) and the equivalence of mass and energy (A.3.e.iii).
Gravitational waves predicted by general relativity and their detection via LIGO address the relationship between gravitational mass and inertial mass in relativistic mechanics (C.3).
Newton's absolute time, Einstein's relativistic spacetime, time dilation, and the block universe concept address kinematics (A.3.a.i) and relativistic mechanics (C).
Einstein's special and general relativity, Brownian motion, and mass-energy equivalence directly address relativistic mechanics (C) and the relationship between gravitational and inertial mass (C.3).
